frames.caption: Build n=3 pairs by appending "(" while open<n and ")" while close<open. Pruning keeps every prefix valid, so all leaves are well-formed — no end filter needed.
frames.note: The decision tree branches into "add (" and "add )" at each step, but the two guards prune every illegal branch before we recurse. What survives is exactly the set of balanced strings, whose count is the n-th Catalan number. Time is O(4^n / sqrt(n)) (Catalan growth), extra space O(n) for the buffer and recursion stack.

frames.caption: open=0 < n=3: CHOOSE "(" -> sb="(". Recurse Backtrack(1, 0).
frames.note: PRUNE-OPEN: we may append "(" only while open < n. An extra "(" beyond n pairs could never be closed inside 2n characters, so that whole subtree is dead — we never even enter it. This is why we build only valid prefixes and need no validity filter at the leaves. After recursing we pop the "(" back off (sb.Length--) so the sibling ")" branch starts from the same prefix — choose, recurse, undo.

frames.caption: open=1 < n=3: CHOOSE "(" -> sb="((". Recurse Backtrack(2, 0).
frames.note: PRUNE-OPEN: we may append "(" only while open < n. An extra "(" beyond n pairs could never be closed inside 2n characters, so that whole subtree is dead — we never even enter it. This is why we build only valid prefixes and need no validity filter at the leaves. After recursing we pop the "(" back off (sb.Length--) so the sibling ")" branch starts from the same prefix — choose, recurse, undo.

frames.caption: open=2 < n=3: CHOOSE "(" -> sb="(((". Recurse Backtrack(3, 0).
frames.note: PRUNE-OPEN: we may append "(" only while open < n. An extra "(" beyond n pairs could never be closed inside 2n characters, so that whole subtree is dead — we never even enter it. This is why we build only valid prefixes and need no validity filter at the leaves. frames.caption: open=3 == n=3: PRUNE "(" — adding another "(" can never be closed in 2n chars. Skip this branch.
frames.note: PRUNE-OPEN: we may append "(" only while open < n. An extra "(" beyond n pairs could never be closed inside 2n characters, so that whole subtree is dead — we never even enter it. This is why we build only valid prefixes and need no validity filter at the leaves. After recursing we pop the "(" back off (sb.Length--) so the sibling ")" branch starts from the same prefix — choose, recurse, undo.

frames.caption: close=0 < open=3: CHOOSE ")" -> sb="((()". Recurse Backtrack(3, 1).
frames.note: PRUNE-CLOSE: we may append ")" only while close < open. Closing more than we have opened makes the prefix unbalanced (e.g. "())") and can never recover, so that branch is pruned. Tracking just two counts — open and close — is enough to enforce well-formedness incrementally. The matching sb.Length-- after recursion rewinds the buffer before returning to the parent.

frames.caption: sb.Length == 2*n: record "((()))" (result #1). Return.
frames.note: BASE CASE: once the buffer reaches 2*n characters it is a complete, guaranteed-valid string (the pruning kept every prefix legal), so we snapshot a COPY into results and return. The number of leaves equals the n-th Catalan number — here C(3)=5.

frames.caption: Done: 5 well-formed strings = Catalan(3). Pruning visited only Catalan(3) leaves, far fewer than 2^(2n) raw branches.
frames.note: There are exactly Catalan(n) balanced strings of n pairs, and the two guards make us touch only those leaves — no invalid prefix is ever extended. You cannot beat this asymptotically because the OUTPUT itself has Catalan(n) ≈ 4^n / (n^1.5 √π) strings, so emitting them all is inherently exponential. Space (excluding output) is O(n) for the buffer plus the recursion stack.
